name: osquery REST API
what_it_does: Open-source endpoint visibility tool that exposes OS data as SQL tables. The Thrift/REST API allows querying endpoints for security data, compliance checks, and threat hunting across fleets.
use_cases: Querying endpoint inventory (running processes, network connections, installed software) for compliance agents
Detecting IoCs (indicators of compromise) by running SQL queries across endpoint fleets
Monitoring for configuration drift vs hardening baselines (CIS benchmarks)
Collecting vulnerability exposure data (OS version, patch level, open ports)
Investigating security incidents via historical scheduled query results
not_for: Real-time blocking/prevention — osquery is read-only and observational
Organizations without a fleet management layer like Fleet or Kolide
best_when: An agent needs structured SQL-queryable access to endpoint OS state for threat hunting, compliance, or incident investigation across many machines.
avoid_when: You need active response capabilities — osquery is read-only. Pair with an EDR for response.

auth.notes: osquery itself uses Thrift API locally. Fleet (the common management layer) exposes REST API with JWT tokens and per-team RBAC. Direct osquery API access is localhost-only for security.

pricing.paid_tiers: Management layers (Fleet Premium, Kolide, Uptycs) add cost
pricing.notes: osquery is free open-source (Apache 2.0). Practical use for fleet management requires a management layer: Fleet (open-source/commercial), Kolide (SaaS), or Uptycs (enterprise).

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted; query performance depends on endpoint hardware and query complexity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SQL-based interface is intuitive. Documentation covers table schemas. Error responses for invalid SQL can be cryptic. Async distributed queries require polling for results across endpoints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All queries are read-only SELECT statements — inherently idempotent and safe to retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Distributed queries are async — must poll for results from offline/slow endpoints
Scheduled queries may have lag — freshness of data depends on collection interval
Some tables are expensive (e.g., hash, file) — queries can overwhelm endpoints
Fleet API is the practical agent interface, not raw osquery Thrift

security_score_components.security_notes: osquery itself is read-only — cannot modify endpoint state. Open-source Apache 2.0. Management layers add auth and RBAC. TLS for daemon communication configurable. Widely deployed by security teams.
